message:
fix(websocket): use semantic close codes and eliminate thundering herd on writer permit release

Two distinct issues addressed:

1. WebSocket close frames previously hardcoded CloseCode::ProtocolError for
   all frame-processing errors, which misrepresents the failure to the peer.
   A PayloadTooLarge error should send MessageTooBig (1009), InvalidUtf8
   should send InvalidPayload (1007), I/O errors should send Abnormal (1006),
   and only actual protocol violations should send ProtocolError (1002).

   New WsError::as_close_code() method (frame.rs) maps each error variant to
   the correct RFC 6455 close code. Client, server, and split read paths all
   now call err.as_close_code() instead of the hardcoded ProtocolError.

2. SplitWritePermit::drop() previously woke ALL queued writer waiters, causing
   a thundering herd where N-1 tasks wake up only to find the permit already
   taken and re-park. Now only the first waiter is woken (FIFO order), and the
   corresponding test is renamed and updated to assert that the second waiter
   is NOT woken.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
